description: Rust idiom review of secretx-aws-ssm crate. Findings filed as child issues under this epic. No correctness bugs found (P0/P1 correctness issues already closed in epic 4as: 4as.1 put error mapping; 6ru.2 put UTF-8 error variant; pde.2 ?field= silent ignore; xyx.3 put ParameterNotFound mapping). All findings here are idiomatic Rust / dependency hygiene / duplication / style.
notes:
## Section 7 — confidence calibration

Probability of missed correctness bug: low (<10%).

Reasoning:
- All P0/P1 correctness issues for this crate are already closed under the prior myoss epic 4as:
  - 4as.1: aws-ssm put() maps all errors to Unavailable — closed
  - 6ru.2: put UTF-8 error uses Backend instead of DecodeFailed — closed
  - pde.2: ?field= silently ignored in get() — closed (now rejected at construction time, line 176-183)
  - xyx.3: put maps ParameterNotFound to Unavailable — closed (now Backend, line 105-117)
- Remaining error-mapping logic was reviewed against aws-sdk-ssm 1.109 modeled error variants:
  - GetParameterError variants: InternalServerError, InvalidKeyId, ParameterNotFound, ParameterVersionNotFound. is_parameter_not_found → NotFound; is_parameter_version_not_found → Backend (correct, see 39da.8); is_invalid_key_id → Backend (correct, KMS misconfig is permanent until operator fixes); AccessDeniedException via meta().code() → Backend (correct, IAM denial is permanent until policy changes); rest → Unavailable. Correct.
  - PutParameterError variants: InternalServerError, InvalidKeyId, ParameterAlreadyExists, ParameterLimitExceeded, ParameterMaxVersionLimitExceeded, ParameterPatternMismatchException, ParameterVersionLimitExceeded, TooManyUpdates, ValidationException, UnsupportedParameterType. Code maps InvalidKeyId, AccessDeniedException, ValidationException → Backend; ParameterNotFound (defensive, can't actually occur with overwrite=true) → Backend; rest → Unavailable.
    Note: ParameterAlreadyExists cannot occur because the call sets overwrite=true. ParameterLimitExceeded and ParameterMaxVersionLimitExceeded are quota errors mapped to Unavailable (transient) — defensible because quota can be raised, though arguably they could be Backend (permanent until quota raised). Same trade-off as 8bh8 8bh8.5 reasoning for aws-sm. NOT filed as a separate finding because it is judgment, not error.
  - The string-match against meta().code() at lines 84-88, 105-108, 125-128, 136-139 catches errors not in the typed enum. Defensible.
- Zeroization story is identical to aws-sm: SDK returns plain String which is in non-zeroizing memory; only the final SecretValue is zeroed on drop. Documented limitation, not a defect.

All 9 findings filed are idiomatic-Rust / dependency-hygiene / duplication / style / defensible-defensive-code. None are correctness.

Comparison to sibling 8bh8 (secretx-aws-sm rusty pass):
- Mirrored: 8bh8.1 → 39da.1 (tokio dep), 8bh8.2 → 39da.2 (zeroize unused), 8bh8.3 → 39da.3 (load_from_env deprecated), 8bh8.5 → 39da.4 (mapper duplication), 8bh8.8 → 39da.6 (Debug derive), 8bh8.9 → 39da.9 (Arc style).
- aws-ssm-only: 39da.5 (is_some_and rewrite — clippy::pedantic confirmed), 39da.7 (backend const), 39da.8 (defend unreachable defensive branch).
- Not applicable to aws-ssm: 8bh8.4 (format!{svc}) — aws-ssm uses e.into() throughout; 8bh8.6/7 (Arc<Client>) — aws-ssm stores aws_sdk_ssm::Client directly without Arc wrapper inside the struct; 8bh8.10 (single_match_else on UTF-8 dispatch) — aws-ssm uses map_err for UTF-8 dispatch, not match.

Confidence raise scenarios:
- Run cargo hack feature-powerset across all-features to confirm no unused dep slipped through.
- Run cargo +<msrv> test to confirm is_some_and rewrite (39da.5) doesn't break MSRV 1.75 (it doesn't — is_some_and is 1.70+).
- Spot-check one downstream consumer for behavioural differences when load_from_env() is replaced with defaults(BehaviorVersion::latest()).
title: epic
